* fix: zero-start EMA seeding to prevent early K_eff spike

Change the EMA seed on a player's first match from `form_ema = delta`
to `form_ema = FORM_EMA_ALPHA * delta`. This is equivalent to starting
the EMA at 0 (neutral form) and applying the standard update rule
consistently from the very first match.

Effect: K_eff at match 2 drops from ~95 to ~46 for a reference 4-0
result (E=0.5), instead of the 2.5× multiplier that the direct-seed
produced. Form influence now grows gradually over 5-10 matches as the
EMA accumulates real evidence.

Applied in both beyblade_elo.py (Xtreme + Combined arena paths) and
elo-calculator.js (frontend calculator).

feat(devreload): expert panel hardening — CompilationFailed event, error overlay, reload guard

Expert panel review identified 10 issues; all addressed:

Must-fix:
- Add CompilationFailed DU case — browser no longer stuck on 'Recompiling...' when FSI eval fails
- Harden SSE exception handling — catch IOException, ObjectDisposedException, InvalidOperationException
- Fix double-unregister — remove IDisposable cleanup, keep only RequestAborted.Register
- Add infinite-reload guard — sessionStorage counter pauses after 3 reloads in 5s

Polish:
- Error overlay (red #dc2626) shows compilation errors directly in browser
- Filename in compiling event: 'Recompiling Handlers.fs...'
- Pre-allocated SSE byte arrays for heartbeat/connected/compiling/reload
- retry:1000 on SSE connect for automatic reconnection
- box-shadow on overlay for visibility on colored backgrounds
- console.debug logging in injected script
- Direct ConcurrentDictionary iteration (no snapshot-to-list allocation)

Tests: 31 total (6 FsCheck property tests + 25 unit tests)
- Property: broadcast delivers to all N clients
- Property: event ordering preserved per client
- Property: unregisterClient idempotent
- Property: registerClient replaces channel
- Property: broadcast with zero clients safe
- Property: DU exhaustiveness (Compiling/Reload/CompilationFailed)
- Lifecycle: Compiling -> CompilationFailed unsticks browser
- Lifecycle: Compiling -> Reload (success path)
- Middleware: injection, idempotency, content-type filtering
- Script: error overlay, reload guard, URL generation

commit.message:
fix: production secrets test to explicitly set defaults when .env present

The test_production_mode_rejects_default_secrets and
test_production_mode_rejects_other_default_secrets tests were fragile because
they relied on jwt_secret/api_secret_key having their Pydantic defaults, but
Pydantic Settings loads .env files which can supply real credentials.

Explicitly pass default values so the tests work regardless of .env content.

commit.message:
fix: wire coerce_numbers through encode numeric paths (#379)

Previously, the coerce_numbers field on EncodeOptions was accepted
by the CLI but never read by the codec. ZonedDecimal, PackedDecimal,
BinaryInt, and EditedNumeric encode paths only accepted Value::String
inputs, silently ignoring Value::Number.

Add coerce_to_str() helper that converts Value::Number to string when
coerce_numbers is enabled. Apply to all four numeric encode functions.

BinaryInt also gains a direct i64 fast-path for Value::Number inputs
(via as_i64()), avoiding the string round-trip.

Add 3 new integration tests:
- encode_coerce_numbers_comp3: COMP-3 with numeric input
- encode_coerce_numbers_binary: BINARY with numeric input
- encode_without_coerce_numbers_rejects_number: negative test

Closes #370

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: SIMD compilation mode - CPU portable vs native mode selection (#67)

This pull request introduces a new SIMD mode configuration system for
the FastPFOR crate, allowing users to select between portable, native,
and runtime SIMD instruction sets for the C++ backend. The changes
improve build flexibility, CI reliability, and documentation clarity,
while updating the crate version and standardizing feature usage in
development scripts.

### SIMD Mode Configuration and Build System

* Added support for selecting SIMD mode (`portable`, `native`, or
`runtime`) via Cargo features and the `FASTPFOR_SIMD_MODE` environment
variable; the build script now warns if multiple SIMD features are
enabled and passes the selected mode to CMake. (`build.rs`,
`Cargo.toml`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-d0d98998092552a1d3259338c2c71e118a5b8343dd4703c0c7f552ada7f9cb42L16-R59)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-2e9d962a08321605940b5a657135052fbcef87b5e360662bb527c96d9a615542R28-R35)
* Updated GitHub Actions CI workflow to test all combinations of OS and
SIMD mode, and to cache builds separately for each SIMD mode.
(`.github/workflows/ci.yml`)

### Documentation Improvements

* Expanded `README.md` with a clear explanation of SIMD modes, their use
cases, and configuration recommendations for end users.

### Development Scripts and Testing

* Standardized the use of the `_all_compatible` feature group in the
`justfile` for building, testing, linting, and coverage, and added
recipes to test all SIMD modes.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L4-R30)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L51-R65)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L95-R130)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-deb9bb56fb122db0b605aa5b63f95a4665c905b18dd670e1fa6c877576a94ff1L130-R145)
* Improved installation logic in the `justfile` for development tools,
with clearer output and better CI compatibility.

### Version Update

* Bumped the crate version from `0.7.0` to `0.8.0` to reflect these
breaking and feature enhancements. (`Cargo.toml`)

commit.message:
feat(analyzer): add H87-H94 heuristics, bump to v3.2.0

New security heuristics from coverage gap analysis:
- H87: TRC curve anomaly detection (excessive points, invalid parametric
  function types >4, NaN/Inf parameters, degenerate all-zero curves)
- H88: Chromatic adaptation matrix validation (undersized chad, singular
  matrix det≈0, NaN/Inf, extreme values >100)
- H89: Profile sequence description validation (excessive entries >256,
  empty sequences, count overflow)
- H90: Preview tag channel consistency (Preview0/1/2 CLUT dimensions
  vs PCS channel count mismatch)
- H91: Colorant order validation (indices OOB vs ColorantTable count,
  duplicate indices)
- H92: Spectral viewing conditions validation (illuminant XYZ NaN/Inf,
  non-positive Y, surround NaN, CCT range)
- H93: Embedded profile flag consistency (reserved bits, conflicting
  embedded/independent flags, attributes reserved bits)
- H94: Matrix/TRC colorant consistency (RGB matrix column sum vs D50
  whitepoint, NaN in columns, negative Y, whitepoint deviation)

Coverage-informed: targets 101 previously-uninspected tag signatures
including TRC, chad, pseq, preview, colorant order, spectral viewing
conditions, and matrix/TRC profile validation.

Verified: 10/10 test profiles pass, findings detected on 12+ CVE PoCs
and crafted profiles across H88/H92/H93/H94.

  a + b c. Unary operators wrap compound operands in parens.

Converter robustness:

  so statement-level exceptions fall back to §CSHARP blocks instead
  of propagating and breaking the entire file conversion.

  total process memory including native allocations (Roslyn, Z3).

  hardcoded 2GB. Still overridable via CALOR_MCP_MAX_MEMORY_MB.

  thundering herd when multiple requests wait simultaneously.

commit.message:
perf(kernels): vectorize softmax exp with AVX2+FMA polynomial (#3354)

## Summary
Replace scalar per-element exp() in both AVX2 softmax hot loops
(softmax.rs + simd_softmax.rs) with a fully vectorized Cephes-style
degree-5 minimax polynomial using FMA intrinsics. This eliminates the
store-to-buffer scalar-loop load-from-buffer anti-pattern that was
defeating SIMD throughput.

## What changed
**Both \softmax.rs\ and \simd_softmax.rs\:**
- **exp_avx2()**: New 8-wide vectorized exp(x) via 2^(xlog2e)
decomposition
  - Cody-Waite two-part ln2 range reduction (preserves precision)
  - Horner evaluation with \_mm256_fmadd_ps\ (5 FMA ops)
  - Bit-manipulation 2^n reconstruction (no libm dependency)
  - Max relative error < 2e-7 over [-88, 88]
- **softmax_avx2()** / **simd_softmax_avx2()**: Now call exp_avx2()
directly zero buffer bouncing

**softmax.rs only:**
- **softmax_avx2_inplace()**: New zero-allocation in-place variant
  - Eliminates data.to_vec() clone in \softmax_f32_inplace()\
- Reads and writes same buffer (each element read once then overwritten)

**All dispatch sites** now check both \vx2\ AND \ ma\ (all Haswell+ /
Kaby Lake CPUs have both)

## Receipt
- **what changed**: crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/softmax.rs (+123/-15),
simd_softmax.rs (+52/-11)
- **what ran locally**:
- \cargo build -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu\
- \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- softmax\ 399 passed
- \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --test avx2_scalar_parity_tests
--no-default-features --features cpu -- softmax\ 4 passed
- \cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- -D warnings\ clean
- **CI relied on**: CI Core (Build & Test, Clippy, BDD Grid Check)
- **parity evidence**: All 4 AVX2-vs-scalar parity tests pass (aligned,
unaligned, stability, pipeline)
- **perf receipt**: No perf claim (structural improvement; benchmark in
follow-up)
- **docs touched**: none
- **remains**: Could add a targeted microbenchmark; vectorized exp could
also benefit layer_norm and attention
- **disposition**: MERGE (A)

## Why this matters
Softmax is called once per attention head per layer per token. For a
32-layer model with 32 heads, that's 1,024 softmax calls per token. The
old code bounced through a scalar buffer for every 8 elements, negating
AVX2 gains. This keeps all computation in vector registers.

commit.message:
feat(apple-silicon): NEON-accelerated attention dot product (#3355)

Implement NEON SIMD fast-path for Q·K^T dot product in the attention
kernel, replacing scalar fallback on ARM64 targets.

## What Changed
- Added `neon_dot`: vectorized dot product using
`vld1q_f32`/`vfmaq_f32`/`vaddvq_f32`
- Added `neon_qk`/`neon_qk_into`: Q·K^T matrix computation using
`neon_dot`
- Updated `dispatch_qk`/`dispatch_qk_into` to auto-select NEON on
aarch64
- Uses baseline ARMv8-A NEON (always available, no runtime detection
needed)

## Expected Impact
~2-4× speedup for attention Q·K^T computation on Apple M-series silicon.

## Verification
- 760 attention tests pass ✅
- `dispatch_qk_matches_scalar` parity test confirms NEON matches
reference ✅
- `cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --features cpu -- -D warnings` ✅
